llm_operation.reason: Both frameworks failed due to invalid target/label values: PyTorch reported 'Target -4 is out of bounds' and Paddle reported 'label value should >= 0 when label value(-6) not equal to ignore_index(-100)'. This indicates the generated target/label tensor contains class indices less than 0 and not equal to ignore_index (-100), violating the constraint that class indices must be in [0, C-1] (C=10 here) or exactly equal to ignore_index. The original test case only specifies shape and dtype but does not constrain the *value range* of the label tensor. To fix, we must ensure all label values are either in [0, 9] (valid class indices for 10 classes) or exactly -100 (ignored). We repair by constraining label values to valid range [0, 9], which satisfies both frameworks' requirements and aligns with doc: PyTorch requires target[i] ∈ [0, C-1] or == ignore_index; Paddle requires lbl >= 0 when soft_label=False and lbl != ignore_index. Since ignore_index is -100, all non-ignored labels must be ≥ 0 — and for integer classification, they must be < C. Thus, safe repair is to generate labels in [0, 9].
